## 1. Macro / Rates / Inflation
Fed held rates at 3.5%-3.75% at January 2026 meeting. Fed officials divided over future rate path—some support further cuts if inflation continues to decline, others prefer holding steady or even raising rates if inflation persists. **MEDIUM materiality** — **CONFIRMS** current positioning: no immediate dovish surprise, VIX-supportive environment continues. ## 2. Geopolitical / Commodity
Oil and geopolitical tensions dominating headlines: U.S.-Iran conflict driving volatility, with oil spikes and subsequent reversals as markets oscillate between panic and resolution signals. ## 3. AI / Semiconductor
Trump administration drafted rules requiring U.S. government approval to ship AI chips anywhere outside the U.S., giving Washington control over global AI training infrastructure buildouts. Proposal reported March 5, 2026; within days, China announced new AI semiconductor subsidy program to reduce dependence on Nvidia/AMD. **HIGH materiality** — **CONTRADICTS** ai_tech BUY signal: proposed export controls increase regulatory uncertainty, could slow hyperscaler CapEx globally. Near-term headwind for SOXQ, CHAT, DTCR. BIS revised license review policy for H200/MI325X from "presumption of denial" to "case-by-case review" effective January 15, 2026, with strict third-party testing and security requirements. DOJ broke up $160M smuggling ring exporting H200 chips to China—same chips now allowed under new framework. **MEDIUM materiality** — **CONFIRMS** structural complexity in semiconductor enforcement; regulatory whipsaw continues. Trump AI Executive Order (Dec 2025) directs federal challenge to state AI laws; DOJ AI Litigation Task Force established Jan 9, 2026; Commerce Dept evaluation due March 11, FTC policy statement due March 11. **MEDIUM materiality** — **NOVEL** but not yet market-priced: federal-state AI regulatory conflict could create compliance fog, particularly for CHAT-type thematic plays.

## 4. Energy / Materials
Uranium outlook bullish for 2026: Sprott highlights renewed contracting, higher prices, and pivotal U.S. policy decisions (Section 232 review, $80B Westinghouse commitment, potential strategic reserve). Spot uranium prices surged ~25% in January 2026, surpassing $100/lb for first time in two years, driven by supply constraints and strong demand. Operational challenges persist: McArthur River lowered 2025 output, Kazatomprom signaled reduced 2026 production, U.S. ISR restarts ramping slower than planned. **HIGH materiality** — **CONFIRMS** strategic_materials (URNM) momentum: supply deficit building, policy tailwinds, AI data center demand structural. AI-driven electricity demand emerging as major driver: 63% of surveyed investors believe AI-related consumption will materially affect nuclear planning over next decade. **MEDIUM materiality** — **CONFIRMS** URNM positioning at top of momentum rankings.

## 7. Rates / Currency
10-year Treasury yield fell >25bp over two weeks (steepest drop since March 2025); inflation-protected TIPS yields fell to late-October 2025 levels; yet gold "doesn't seem to care"—negative correlation with real rates has broken.
